utility_evaluation.REASONING: There are 5 items in the required format: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. Now compare with the final plan: it includes health_coverage (with physical and mental and explicit costs), property_coverage (with cost), travel_coverage (with cost), additional_services (but presented as a description without an explicit cost), and total_budget (monthly and annual costs). Next, check if each is fully specified with all required details: The format requires each category to include costs. Health has costs for both physical and mental—fully specified. Property has cost—fully specified. Travel has cost—fully specified. Additional_services requires 'any enhancement services with costs'—the plan lists the service but provides no cost, so it is not fully specified. Total_budget requires complete monthly/annual cost breakdown—provided. Therefore, 4 out of 5 items are fully specified.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan includes four specific items: LifeFirst Standard Plan for Physical Health, LifeFirst Standard Plan for Mental Health, LifeFirst Standard Coverage for Home Content, and LifeFirst Standard Travel Plan. In the ground-truth ratings, these correspond to: Primary health coverage -> physical_health -> LifeFirst Standard Plan (rating 9); Primary health coverage -> mental_health -> LifeFirst Standard Plan (rating 9); Property_protection -> home_content -> LifeFirst Standard Coverage (rating 9); Mobility_coverage -> travel_insurance -> LifeFirst Standard Plan (rating 9). Therefore, each selected LifeFirst item maps to a rating of 9.
